'''Phil Hassan''' (born 18 August 1974) is a former [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played club level rugby league (RL) for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], as a {{rlp|wg}}, or {{rlp|ce}}, and club level rugby union (RU) for [[Worcester Warriors|Worcester]] and [[Fylde Rugby Club|Fylde]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/Phil-Hassan/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>

==Leeds career==
Hassan was born in [[Wigan]] on 18 August 1974. Hassan joined [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in August 1993, making his début in his first season. Hassan joined from Wigan-based amateur club [[Wigan St Pat's|St Pat]]'s. Hassan played against his home town club in the crushing Premiership Trophy Final of 1995. Hassan was a regular in the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] first team squad over the next three seasons but his big break through came at the start of Super League when he made 24 appearances and scored four tries. Hassan established himself in the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] first team and kicked on the following season, missing only one game and scoring 11 tries, including a spectacular long range try in both games against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] that season. In total, Hassan played 71 times for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] scoring 16 tries, however the arrival of [[Brad Godden]] meant there was no place for Hassan at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]].

==Rugby career==
Hassan moved on from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at the end of the 1997 season, switching codes to play rugby union with [[Worcester Warriors|Worcester]] [[Rugby Union|(RU)]]. Hassan returned to rugby league with [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] before joining [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in 2000. At the end of the 2001 season, Hassan was released by the [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], and again switched codes to join [[Fylde Rugby Club|Fylde]] [[Rugby Union|(RU)]], in April 2002 again returned to rugby league joining [[Wakefield Trinity]], and later [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]].
